LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT HOME REALTY INVESTMENT & CONSTRUCTION, ANTONIO CASTELLUCCI, MARIA CASTELLUCCI, RITA CASTELLUCCI NTC OF MTN AND SPECIAL MTN TO STRIKE ANTI-SLAPP LAWSUIT AND REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE- MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING ELLIS ACT NOTICE COULD BE PROTECTED CONDUCT, DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THIS CASE ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY BECAUSE IT IS PREMISED ON DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED CONDUCT IN WRONGFULLY EVICTING PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR TO JUNE 2006. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES PURSUANT TO C C P 425.16 (C) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO ANSWER COMPLAINT FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. Notice And Motion To Quash Subpoena For Records From Brown & Company
Off Calendar Aug-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Philip Bonoli, Esq., appeared (via telephone) for Defendant & moving party: Home Realty Investment & Construction
Ariel Gershon, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Robert Camyre

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Home Realty Investment & Construction, Antonio Castellucci, Maria Castellucci, Rita Castellucci's Motion To Quash Subpoena For Records From Brown & Company is granted. The subpoena is overly broad. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Apr-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. STEVEN J. MCDONALD OF GREENSTEIN & MCDONALD, TEL. NO. 773-1240 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PHILIP J. BONOLI OF SOLTMAN, LEVITT & FLAHERTY, TEL. NO. (805)-497-7706 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT HOME REALTY INVESTMENT & CONSTRUCTION, ANTONIO CASTELLUCCI, MARIA CASTELLUCCI, RITA CASTELLUCCI Ntc Of Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Anti-Slapp Lawsuit And Request for Judicial Notice- MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING ELLIS ACT NOTICE COULD BE PROTECTED CONDUCT, DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THIS CASE ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY BECAUSE IT IS PREMISED ON DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED CONDUCT IN WRONGFULLY EVICTING PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR TO JUNE 2006. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES PURSUANT TO C C P 425.16 (C) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO ANSWER COMPLAINT FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/JF
